title: coccocypselum macropodum
intro: Coccocypselum macropodum, also known as the Large-leafed Peppermint Tree or Large-leaved Peppermint Bush, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to South America and the Caribbean. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of essential oils such as limonene and beta-pinene. Consuming Coccocypselum macropodum has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, although its use and preparation may vary depending on the region and intended application.
